message: Resolver gate open — awaiting human decisions on gating cards

gating.title: Egress PHI Redaction Scope Unspecified
gating.detail: The PRD states 'PII redaction at egress' but does not enumerate which FHIR Observation fields constitute PHI/PII requiring redaction, nor whether patient identifiers (subject reference, encounter ID) are stripped or pseudonymized before reaching the clinical event bus.
gating.gap_description: The PRD states 'PII redaction at egress' but does not enumerate which FHIR Observation fields constitute PHI/PII requiring redaction, nor whether patient identifiers (subject reference, encounter ID) are stripped or pseudonymized before reaching the clinical event bus.

options.label: Pseudonymize Direct Identifiers per HIPAA Safe Harbor
options.resolution: At egress, replace FHIR Observation.subject (patient reference) and Observation.encounter with a deterministic HMAC-SHA256 token keyed by a rotating secret. All 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers (§164.514(b)(2)) present in the resource are either removed or tokenized. A mapping table is retained in a HIPAA-compliant datastore accessible only to authorized clinical systems.
options.rationale: HIPAA §164.514(b) Safe Harbor de-identification requires removal or transformation of all 18 identifier categories; pseudonymization preserves re-linkability for clinical continuity while satisfying minimum-necessary (§164.502(b)).
options.downstream_impact: Architect will design a tokenization service and secure mapping store; CodeGen will implement an egress transformation pipeline that applies HMAC tokens before publishing to the event bus.

options.label: Strip All Patient Identifiers Entirely
options.resolution: Remove Observation.subject, Observation.encounter, and any contained patient resource from the egress payload entirely, publishing only device ID, timestamp, and vital-sign values.
options.rationale: Full removal guarantees no PHI leaves the ingest boundary and simplifies compliance review; acceptable if downstream Cardiology consumers can re-correlate via device-to-patient mapping they already maintain.
options.downstream_impact: Architect must confirm Cardiology event bus consumers have an independent device-to-patient correlation mechanism; CodeGen implements a simpler field-drop transform with no tokenization infrastructure.

gating.title: Vendor Connector Authorization Model Undefined
gating.detail: No concrete authentication/authorization mechanism is specified for Philips IntelliVue and GE CARESCAPE connectors, leaving the trust boundary and credential management approach undefined.
gating.prd_quote: Authorization model and egress policy for vendor connectors are TBD — some teams have been connecting to unapproved external tools without formal review.
gating.gap_description: No concrete authentication/authorization mechanism is specified for Philips IntelliVue and GE CARESCAPE connectors, leaving the trust boundary and credential management approach undefined.

options.label: Mutual TLS + OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials per Vendor
options.resolution: Each vendor connector must authenticate via mutual TLS (client certificate issued by internal PKI) combined with OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant scoped to 'vitals:ingest'. Certificates rotate every 90 days. No connector may be activated without a signed BAA and Security Architecture approval ticket.
options.rationale: HIPAA §164.312(d) requires entity authentication; mTLS + OAuth satisfies both transport-layer and application-layer identity requirements and creates an auditable approval gate preventing the unapproved-connection pattern noted in the PRD.
options.downstream_impact: Architect will design a vendor-connector registry service; CodeGen will implement certificate provisioning hooks and OAuth token validation middleware for each WebSocket upgrade handshake.

options.label: API Key per Vendor with IP Allowlist
options.resolution: Each vendor is issued a long-lived API key stored in the secrets manager; inbound connections are additionally restricted to a vendor-supplied static IP range registered in the firewall allowlist.
options.rationale: Lower implementation complexity and compatible with vendors that do not support OAuth; acceptable under HIPAA §164.312(d) if keys are rotated on compromise and access is logged.
options.downstream_impact: Architect will design an API-key validation layer and firewall rule management process; CodeGen will implement key-lookup middleware, but mTLS termination infrastructure is not required.

gating.title: Data Retention Period for Ingested Vitals Not Defined
gating.detail: The PRD does not specify how long raw or normalized vital-sign events (which contain PHI) are retained in the ingest pipeline, event bus, or any intermediate store before deletion or archival.
gating.gap_description: The PRD does not specify how long raw or normalized vital-sign events (which contain PHI) are retained in the ingest pipeline, event bus, or any intermediate store before deletion or archival.

options.label: 6-Year Retention with Encrypted Archival, Purge on Schedule
options.resolution: Raw and normalized PHI-bearing events are retained in hot storage for 7 days (for replay/debugging), then archived to AES-256-encrypted cold storage for 6 years from creation date, then automatically purged. Retention schedule is enforced by a scheduled deletion job audited quarterly. Aligns with HIPAA §164.530(j) medical record retention minimum.
options.rationale: HIPAA §164.530(j) requires covered entities to retain documentation for 6 years from creation or last effective date; applying this to the underlying PHI records is the conservative, audit-safe interpretation.
options.downstream_impact: Architect will design a tiered storage architecture (hot → cold) with lifecycle policies; CodeGen will implement automated archival jobs, encryption-at-rest for cold store, and a deletion audit log.

options.label: 7-Day Hot Retention Only, No Archival
options.resolution: Normalized events are retained in the event bus for 7 days for consumer replay, then permanently deleted. No cold archival. The authoritative clinical record is assumed to be written by downstream EHR systems, not this pipeline.
options.rationale: If this pipeline is a transport layer only and the EHR is the system of record, retaining PHI beyond operational needs violates HIPAA minimum-necessary (§164.502(b)); shorter retention reduces breach exposure surface.
options.downstream_impact: Architect designs a simpler single-tier event bus with TTL; CodeGen implements TTL-based deletion only, but the team must formally document that EHR is the system of record to justify this retention policy to the Privacy Office.

gating.title: WebSocket Session Authentication Mechanism Unspecified
gating.detail: The PRD specifies WebSocket as the transport but does not define how WebSocket sessions are authenticated for internal consumers (e.g., Cardiology engineering team), leaving the session establishment security model undefined.
gating.gap_description: The PRD specifies WebSocket as the transport but does not define how WebSocket sessions are authenticated for internal consumers (e.g., Cardiology engineering team), leaving the session establishment security model undefined.

options.label: Short-Lived JWT Bearer Token in HTTP Upgrade Header
options.resolution: WebSocket connections must present a signed JWT (RS256, max TTL 15 minutes) issued by the internal identity provider in the HTTP Upgrade request's Authorization header. The gateway validates the token before completing the 101 handshake. Tokens must include 'vitals:subscribe' scope and a subject claim matching an approved service account.
options.rationale: HIPAA §164.312(d) requires unique user/entity authentication; short-lived JWTs prevent credential replay attacks and are compatible with WebSocket upgrade flows per RFC 6455 without requiring custom protocol extensions.
options.downstream_impact: Architect will specify an IDP integration and token-validation middleware at the gateway; CodeGen will implement JWT validation on WebSocket upgrade, token-refresh logic for long-lived connections, and scope enforcement.

options.label: Session Cookie Issued After OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
options.resolution: Clients complete an OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE against the internal IDP; the resulting session cookie is presented on the WebSocket Upgrade request and validated by the gateway.
options.rationale: Preferred for browser-based or user-facing clients where the authorization code flow provides stronger phishing resistance; acceptable under HIPAA §164.312(d) if session cookies are HttpOnly, Secure, and have a short max-age.
options.downstream_impact: Architect must design a cookie-based session store and CSRF protection; CodeGen implements cookie validation middleware, but JWT infrastructure is not required, reducing token-signing key management overhead.

gating.title: Ingest Latency SLA Measurement Point Undefined
gating.detail: The PRD does not define where the 100ms latency is measured (bedside monitor → API gateway, API gateway → event bus publish acknowledgment, or end-to-end to Cardiology consumer), making the SLA unenforceable and untestable.
gating.gap_description: The PRD does not define where the 100ms latency is measured (bedside monitor → API gateway, API gateway → event bus publish acknowledgment, or end-to-end to Cardiology consumer), making the SLA unenforceable and untestable.

options.label: Measure Gateway Receipt to Event Bus Publish ACK
options.resolution: The <100ms SLA is defined as p99 latency from WebSocket frame receipt at the API gateway to confirmed publish acknowledgment from the clinical event bus broker, measured continuously via distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry spans). Breaches trigger a PagerDuty P2 alert.
options.rationale: This boundary is fully within the team's control, aligns with standard SRE practices for owned-service SLOs, and provides a deterministic measurement point independent of vendor network variability.
options.downstream_impact: Architect will specify OpenTelemetry instrumentation points at gateway ingress and broker publish; CodeGen will instrument WebSocket handler and event-bus client with trace spans and expose a latency histogram metric.

options.label: Measure End-to-End Bedside Monitor to Event Bus
options.resolution: The <100ms SLA is defined as p99 latency from the timestamp embedded in the vendor device payload to event bus publish ACK, requiring NTP-synchronized clocks on all bedside monitors.
options.rationale: Provides a clinically meaningful latency guarantee that includes vendor connector processing time, which may matter for time-sensitive cardiac events.
options.downstream_impact: Architect must include clock-sync requirements in vendor onboarding; CodeGen must parse and validate device-side timestamps and handle clock-skew detection, increasing implementation complexity.

payload.architecture:
# Solution Architecture: Real-Time Vitals Ingestion Platform

---

## Components & Data Flow

- **WebSocket API Gateway** — Terminates WebSocket connections from downstream clinical consumers. Validates RS256-signed JWT (≤15 min TTL, `vitals:subscribe` scope, approved service-account subject) *before* completing the HTTP 101 handshake; rejects upgrades on any token failure. *(Decision: WebSocket JWT auth)*

- **Vendor Connector Layer** — One connector process per vendor, each authenticated via mTLS (internal PKI, 90-day cert rotation) + OAuth 2.0 `client_credentials` grant scoped to `vitals:ingest`. Connector activation is gated on a signed BAA and a Security Architecture approval ticket stored in the service registry. *(Decision: mTLS + OAuth vendor auth)*

- **PHI De-identification Sidecar** — Runs as an in-process or co-located sidecar on each connector. At egress, replaces `Observation.subject` and `Observation.encounter` with a deterministic HMAC-SHA256 token (rotating secret, managed via secrets manager). All remaining 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers are removed or tokenized before any message leaves the connector boundary. *(Decision: HMAC tokenization at egress)*

- **Toke

message: Gate 2 (Design Review) — human review in v1

payload.test_plan:
# Contract Tests

---

## Contract Test 1

**Given** a valid request payload is sent to the API endpoint
**When** the consumer submits a POST request with all required fields
**Then** the provider returns a `201 Created` status code with the expected response schema containing the required fields

---

## Contract Test 2

**Given** the consumer expects a specific response structure from the provider
**When** a GET request is made to retrieve a resource by ID
**Then** the provider returns a `200 OK` response with a body that matches the agreed-upon JSON schema, including all mandatory fields and correct data types

---

## Contract Test 3

**Given** an invalid or malformed request is sent to the provider
**When** the consumer submits a request with missing required fields
**Then** the provider returns a `400 Bad Request` status code with an error response body matching the agreed error schema

---

## Contract Test 4

**Given** a resource does not exist on the provider side
**When** the consumer sends a GET request for a non-existent resource ID
**Then** the provider returns a `404 Not Found` status code with an error message body conforming to the agreed contract

---

## Contract Test 5

**Given** the consumer is authenticated with a valid token
**When** a DELETE request is made to remove an existing resource
**Then** the provider returns a `204 No Content` status code with no response body, confirming the deletion contract is honored

payload.code:
```python
"""
Contract tests for the Real-Time Vitals Ingestion Platform.

Tests cover:
1. POST /observations -> 201 Created
2. GET /observations/{id} -> 200 OK with schema
3. POST /observations (invalid) -> 400 Bad Request
4. GET /observations/{non-existent-id} -> 404 Not Found
5. DELETE /observations/{id} -> 204 No Content
"""

import hashlib
import hmac
import json
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Optional
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class Observation:
    """Represents a de-identified FHIR Observation resource."""

    REQUIRED_FIELDS = {"resourceType", "id", "status", "code", "subject", "effectiveDateTime"}

    def __init__(
        self,
        resource_type: str,
        obs_id: str,
        status: str,
        code: dict,
        subject: str,
        effective_datetime: str,
        encounter: Optional[str] = None,
        value: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.resourceType = resource_type
        self.id = obs_id
        self.status = status
        self.code = code
        self.subject = subject
        self.effectiveDateTime = effective_datetime
        self.encounter = encounter
        self.value = value

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Serialize to dictionary, omitting None values."""
        data: dict[str, Any] = {
            "resourceType": self.resourceType,
            "id": self.id,
            "status": self.status,
            "code": self.code,
            "subject": self.subject,
            "effectiveDateTime": self.effectiveDateTime,
        }
        if self.encounter is not None:
            data["encounter"] = self.encounter
        if self.value is not None:
            data["value"] = self.value
        return data

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: dict) -> "Observation":
        """Deserialize from dictionary."""
        return cls(
            resource_type=data["resourceType"],
            obs_id=data["id"],
            status=data["status"],
            code=data["code"],
            subject=data["subject"],
            effective_datetime=data["effectiveDateTime"],
            encounter=data.get("encounter"),
            value=data.get("value"),
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PHI De-identification Sidecar
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PHIDeidentificationSidecar:
    """
    Replaces PHI identifiers with deterministic HMAC-SHA256 tokens.
    Implements HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification at connector egress.
    """

    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
        self._secret = secret

    def tokenize(self, value: str) -> str:
        """Return a deterministic HMAC-SHA256 hex token for *value*."""
        return hmac.new(self._secret, value.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()

    def deidentify(self, observation: dict) -> dict:
        """
        Replace subject and encounter with HMAC tokens and strip remaining
        HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers.
        """
        result = dict(observation)
        if "subject" in result:
            result["subject"] = self.tokenize(result["subject"])
        if "encounter" in result:
            result["encounter"] = self.tokenize(result["encounter"])
        # Strip any remaining direct identifiers (Safe Harbor fields)
        phi_fields = {
            "patientName", "birthDate", "address", "phone", "fax",
            "email", "ssn", "mrn", "accountNumber", "certificateNumber",
            "vehicleId", "deviceId", "url", "ip", "biometric", "photo",
            "geolocation", "dateOfDeath",
        }
        for field in phi_fields:
            result.pop(field, None)
        return result


# -----------------------------
